lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhm... any idea on where I should start looking for your daughter?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: There is a large pool of water beyond the camp. She used to go there often, it was her favourite spot.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Ragnar, her fiance, is probably there as well. I will give him my permission to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does your daughter look like?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: She is the fairest maiden of all our tribe, and I daresay of all the northern lands. She has flowing black hair, and when last we saw her, she was wearing a brown dress.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Perhaps you should speak to the man she is to marry, tribesman Ragnar. He can probably tell you more.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You can find him out by the pool beyond the camp, where my daughter used to go so often.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings, outerlander, I am Hamal. I am the chieftain of this tribe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And my name is [player name/Fremennik name], honourable chieftain.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, an outerlander with manners.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: So tell me what you want from us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm a Fremennik, not an outerlander.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We are not Fremennik, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you look like Fremennik!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: And you do not look like a Fremennik, but still claim to be one.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: But I will not deceive you. We were part of the Fremennik once, but left the village of Rellekka long ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fascinating, but I'm afraid I have other things to do at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is everyone being so hostile to me?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We are a solitary group, outerlander, and have little experience or interest in outside contact.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: It is also our custom not to talk to outerlanders without my sanction. And I see no reason to sanction it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you lot all doing up here in the mountains?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We left Rellekka because we wanted to live closer to nature. We feel that the people of Rellekka have grown complacent and rely too much on their tools rather than their skill. And while they share our view on the sanctity

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: of the runestones, they still employ and actively use magic.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Their Seer is an affront to the gods!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you haven't even seen what he did to his house!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Anyway.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We do not recognise the council of Rellekka and want nothing more to do with them. We live on rock and stone now, pure and hard.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We move on to another part of the mountain slope when the rough land cannot sustain us anymore.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: That said, we have tarried at this location for too long and should be moving on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why are you still here?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: For one thing, this side of the mountain is mostly free from the menace of the trolls and the delvings of the deformed dwarves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, you see...

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: My daughter has gone missing. We have been looking for her and cannot go on without her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A damsel in distress! I will search for her!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Haha, you have spirit, [player name/fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: But we do not employ outerlanders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't need to employ me! Just let me look for her.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmmm.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: That seems acceptable to me. Very well, I will allow you to look for her.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: I will instruct my people to let you roam about the area freely, and give limited consent to them to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! I'll be back with her soon. Just wait here.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Oh, I'll be waiting here alright.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We do not need your sympathy, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the trolls got her?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Do not say such things in jest, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Besides, stealth and kidnapping is not their way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it then, you can figure it out on your own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'll stop talking to you then.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: As you wish, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a quest! Do you have any for me?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We do not employ outerlanders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I know the drill, where do I sign up for the trials to join your tribe?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: And we certainly don't let them join us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! I'm trying to help your chieftain here.

lines.speaker: Camp dweller
lines.text: We don't need any help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there! Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Camp dweller
lines.text: Stay away from me, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I need some help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, yes...

lines.speaker: Camp dweller
lines.text: Run along then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I'm-

lines.speaker: Camp dweller
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't there-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you come and help me? The chieftain's daughter-

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: No, outerlander, I'm guarding this mountain pass from trolls and inquisitive people like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't look like you were very successful in the last regard.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: If the chieftain would let me, I would correct that oversight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea where the chieftain's daughter is?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: That's what we would all like to know.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: So, you are the outerlander whom Hamal said would look for Asleif.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: You don't even know her name!

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I am sorry outerlander, I have not spoken with your kind for a long time and I'm not feeling so well. You see, I was set to marry Asleif, the most beautiful woman ever to grace this world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea where she might be?

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: She used to come here every day, you know, whenever our camp was set up in the vicinity. She would come to sing, her voice reflecting on the mountains and falling into the pool like sparkling silver. Sometimes I think I can hear her singing, but when I follow the sound around the pool I just end up here again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... do you have any idea where she might be now?

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: We were supposed to meet here, at the tree by the pool. I think if I wait long enough maybe she will show up again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... you don't actually have any idea where she is right at the moment?

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: If I knew I wouldn't be standing here, would I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean, my kind? I'm a Fremennik you know.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Yet you still use magic, I assume, and have little actual knowledge of the earth and the rock that you stand on every day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose...

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Then you are an ignorant, an insult to the gods... and an outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, you made your point!

sections.title: Listening to the outer shining pools
Attempting to climbing the slippery tree
Using the mud on the tree
Jumping across the clump of rocks
Using the plank on the flat stones
Listening to the center shining pool

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yes, hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'm going mad, I'm starting to hear things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! Who are you? And where for that matter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose that answered both questions then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, you're Asleif? I think I was looking for you! Where are you hiding?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I thought you said you were Asleif?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'd better have a lie down, I'm starting to get a little dizzy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly do you want from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I don't think they like the idea of me helping them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All right, that sounds like something I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So let's see, peace with Rellekka, and a new supply of food.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I'm not really a farmer. You'll have to find someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I'm not really a diplomat. You'll have to find someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't tell me, there's an anthem involved?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I spoke to Asleif!

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Really? You found her? Where is she, tell me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... there's something you should know.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, she's sort of... well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's sort of dead.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: What? Do not say such things! Tell me where you saw her!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't actually see her, not as such. But I did talk to her, right there, on that island in the center of the pool.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: That island is bare! There's no one there! Do not try to deceive me, outerlander, she is not dead and you did not talk to her!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, don't believe me then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have found your daughter!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Where is she? Tell me, outerlander!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, er, she is dead.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Do not play games with me. I gave you leave to walk about our camp, not to trick us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the people of Rellekka...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you hate them so much?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We do not hate them, outerlander, but we do not approve of their ways either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But don't you basically believe in the same things?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: It is true that our beliefs are not all that different, but we have a different way of applying them, a stricter way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've never considered, say, making peace with them?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We are not at war with them, outerlander, but we try to ignore each other.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: But I must admit that I have, in fact, sent out a representative to Rellekka to talk about improving relations and perhaps setting up a basic trade route.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Well, that was easy.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Unfortunately we have not heard from Svidi, our representative, for a few days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I go looking for him as well then?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Do as you wish, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're all such a group of petty bickerers! I've only just gone and made peace between Miscellania and Etceteria!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Miscellania? So that group survived?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, do you know them?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Oh yes... that group left the mainland a long time ago. And good riddance to them, I say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't tell me you're fighting them as well.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Oh, we're not fighting anyone. But we cannot forgive them for their insulting behaviour before they left.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: It is quite inappropriate to pray to the gods when the moon is half full. Yet it is almost as if they wanted to make a point of it, holding parties in honour of gods almost every night.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: That, and they like to dress up in fancy clothes a bit too much to our tastes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on then, go on, tell me, who else split off from your little group of infighting barbarians?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: How do you mean? There is no one else. Well, there's the Gunnarian people who left this area over a century ago, but they don't really count, do they?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: And I suppose that the people of-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! Forget it! I didn't really want to hear all that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About your food supplies...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From what I understand you're starting to run out?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Where did you get that idea from, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I just happened to overhear it.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: There is some truth to it, yes. If it were not for my missing daughter, we would have moved on already, and finding food would be easier again for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help you with finding a new supply of food?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: I would be interested to see how you would manage that, outerlander. But if you insist on trying, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Go, outerlander, and do not hurry back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you, by any chance, Svidi? Sent by Hamal to talk to Brundt and the council of Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Maybe... who is asking? You're an outerlander, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but Hamal asked me to rescue you from a Rellekka prison. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: I haven't actually been in Rellekka yet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, can't you find the way?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Well, I'm not actually sure if I should go in there, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: I've always been told that the people of Rellekka are ignorants, almost as bad as other outerlanders. I think they might kill me if I go there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nonsense. I'm an outerlander and I haven't killed you either!

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Yes, but you're on your own, they have a whole village.

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: So I don't want to go in there, but I don't want to go back to the camp and tell Hamal that I didn't dare to go to Rellekka either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you supposed to be a ferocious warrior?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Not all of us are warriors.

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Some of us are schooled in the fine art of diplomacy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I persuade you to go in there somehow?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: I won't go in there unless I know it's safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want me to go with you as your bodyguard?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: You? But you're an outerlander! You'd probably lead me to a cave to kill me yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I get you some kind of guarantee of safe passage from the village? Will that do?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Hmmmm...

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Yes, that will do.

sections.title: Talking to Svidi before receiving the safety guarantee

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weren't you on your way to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Weren't you on your way to get me a guarantee of safe passage into Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I almost forgot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brundt doesn't want to talk to any representative until your tribe gives back some rock or other.

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: In that case I'm staying right here. I don't have the authority to trade away our rock.

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Besides, it's ours and he can't have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The chieftain from the mountain camp has sent a representative to Rellekka!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Very unusual, outerlander, but we have not seen him yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's the problem, he doesn't dare to come into Rellekka. He's afraid he's going to get hurt.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: What? He's not much of a warrior, is he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I thought. He says he wants a guarantee of safety.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I will meet with this man on one condition, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: When their group split off from Rellekka, they stole one of the ancient relics of the Fremennik. Our ancient rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ancient rock? What did it do?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did it contain vast magical powers?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did it give the owner unimaginable strength?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was it the eternal prison of an immortal demon?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Amusing, [Fremennik name], but as a Fremennik yourself you know better than that.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No, no, no. Little do you understand our ways, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The rock was just that, a rock. It had no special properties and was in essence no different from any other rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did it mean?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It symbolised our refusal to use the runestones for ourselves, instead relying on our own abilities.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The people you speak of took the stone with them when they left for the mountains. They claimed their way of living was more pure and closer to nature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And is that true?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Maybe, but where's the harm in a bit of feasting from time to time?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: In any case, before the council and I meet with anyone from their camp, they will have to give our rock back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did it look pretty?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmm, not especially I think...

sections.title: Talking to Brundt before receiving the rock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why won't you meet with the representative of the mountain camp?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Like I said, we don't want anything to do with them until they give back our ancient rock.

sections.title: Returning into the camp through the rockslide

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I suppose I might as well let you pass now, since I can't seem to stop you anyway.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Just remember that I'm watching you.

sections.title: Talking to Hamal before removing the rock

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: What is it, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the people of Rellekka...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chieftain Brundt says that he won't meet with Svidi unless you give him back some rock or other.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: What, do you mean our ancient rock that our fathers took with them when they left the ignorants of Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's the one.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Out of the question. That rock belongs to us. It symbolises what we stand for. What would Brundt want with it anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can't persuade you to part with the rock?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Did you not hear me the first time, outerlander? It belongs to us, we will not part with it.

sections.title: Removing the half a rock from the Ancient Rock
Exiting tent with half a rock

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What do you have there, outerlander? It's not our rock, is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, of course not! See? It's a completely different rock, it's much smaller.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Alright, you be on your way then.

sections.title: Talking to Brundt the Chieftain after obtaining half a rock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've brought you back your rock!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Really? Let me see!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This rock seems a lot smaller than our bard made it out to be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's not the whole rock, it's part of the rock...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I split it in two.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You truly have the Fremennik spirit in you, [player name]

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You are unusually insightful for an outerlander!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes, we are satisfied with sharing the rock. I'll meet with this representative and will inform the council to join us here in the longhall.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Although I see that Manni is already here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have that guarantee of safe passage, then, to give to the representative?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have that guarantee of safe passage, then, to give to the representative?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Oh yes, here it is.

sections.title: Returning to Svidi with the safety guarantee

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brundt gave me the guarantee of safe passage to give to you!

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Let me see that, I want to be sure it's authentic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Hmmm...

lines.speaker: Svidi
lines.text: Okay, this looks valid enough. I'll go and talk to Brundt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: What is it, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it, I persuaded Brundt to speak to your representative!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Indeed, outerlander, and how did you accomplish that without giving him our rock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, yeah, I need to talk to you about that...

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Worry not, you made a wise decision and your boldness is impressive.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: It does not matter, after all, how large the rock is, or what its shape is. And perhaps the two halves of the rock will have extra symbolism now, representing the two halves of the Fremennik culture.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: But first things first. Did Svidi go into Rellekka yet, to speak to Brundt on behalf of our tribe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops, I completely forgot about that. I'd better hurry back to him.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: I understand that Svidi's meeting with Brundt went quite well. I must thank you for your assistance, outerlander.

sections.title: Talking to Hamal before obtaining the white pearl

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for some bushes that carry White Pearl fruit. Do you know where I can find any?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Weren't you the one who was supposed to be looking for it for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How goes the goat herding?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: It goes, outerlander...

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: Times are hard here at the moment. We've been here for too long and there is not much food left, or places where I can take my goats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm trying to find a new supply of food for your people. Any ideas on where to start?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: If only it were so easy!

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: That being said...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes..?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: I have heard there is a certain kind of fruit... that will grow even here, in the mountains.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the name of the fruit?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: It is called White Pearl, named after its white appearance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on where to find some?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: I do not know. I believe it grows mainly in the mountains, and it can grow in even the most difficult circumstances.

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: We cannot move on until we find the chieftain's daughter, and we do not generally move far inland. But perhaps you can search for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where could I find that fruit again?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: You mean the White Pearl?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: I do not know, outerlander, but you might want to try looking on mountains.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about this one?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: No, if it grew on here I would have known.

sections.title: Talking to Jokul after obtaining the white pearl

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I found the White Pearl fruit!

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: You seem to be quite resourceful, outerlander. Go and show it to the chieftain.

sections.title: Talking to Hamal after obtaining the white pearl

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: What is it, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I have something that might interest you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's White Pearl fruit, which grows in even the hardiest circumstances. I found this growing on White Wolf Mountain.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: And what use is one piece of fruit to us, outerlander? It won't feed our tribe for even a day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops, I hadn't thought of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found this seed that may be of interest to you.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Interesting, what is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the seed of the White Pearl fruit. It grows on White Wolf mountain, even in the snow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With luck, you'll be able to grow it here as well.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: This is a great gift, [player name/fremennik name], which I humbly accept.

sections.title: Talking to Jokul after giving Hamal the white pearl seed

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: I must thank you for your generous gift, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gift?

lines.speaker: Jokul
lines.text: Yes, the seed of the White Pearl. It grows remarkably well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... well, glad to be of help.

sections.title: Talking to Hamal before fighting the Kendal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hamal, I'm sorry, but your daughter is dead.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: I told you before not to say such things.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: What proof of it do you have anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ehm, none really...

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Then do not say it again. I appreciate what you've done for us already, but don't try our patience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, ehm... Asleif spirit thing? Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did what you asked me to! I improved the relations between Rellekka and the mountain camp, and I found a new supply of food!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you have any other quests for me, perhaps with some bigger rewards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I do that, exactly? I don't think I'll be able to get him down to this little island.

sections.title: Attacking him before talking to him

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Do not attack me, puny mortal [player name/fremennik name]!

sections.title: Trying to pick up the corpse of woman before killing him

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I did not give you permission to touch that, mortal!

sections.title: Trying to kill the Kendal if it belongs to another player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr... hello?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Who dares to disturb my slumber?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just me, [player name/fremennik name], no one special.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Have you come, then, to pay your respects?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How... exactly do I do that?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: With your blood, mortal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Time to go, see you later!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Go now, mortal, but I'll be watching you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean a sacrifice? Are you some kind of god?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I am the guardian of the mountain, the god of the northern lands. I move the earth and control the skies. I am the Kendal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek your advice, oh great one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek the cowardly murderer of a young lady of a nearby clan of mountain people. Do you know where I can find this person?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I am not here to answer questions of such trivial nature! Go now before doom befalls you, mortal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You look like you escaped from a fairground!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I would not say such things if I were you, mortal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You look like a man in a bearsuit!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: What did you say, mortal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I said, you look like a man wearing a bearsuit!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: How did you figure that out? How did you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just said you looked like a man wearing a bearsuit, I didn't know you were!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you doing here, pretending to be a god?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Ah, the people that live in the mountains here, they are a superstitious lot. They fear few things, but magic and gods they're scared to death of.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: So I took on the guise of one of their legends. It's a good way of being left alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And do you leave them alone as well?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Ah, you're referring to the skeletons you see here? Yes, I did. Most of the time, at least. When there was no troll to be ambushed, and I had to eat...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you kill a young lady, by the lake? Just recently?

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Recently? Oh no, the entrance you came through has been blocked off for a good many years now. I've only seen trolls and the occasional stray dwarf since then, until you came along.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: But yes, more than ten years ago, I did kill someone like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, never mind then, that can't be the person I'm looking for. I'll be off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I see that corpse? I'd like to make sure of something.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: She is just over there, in the centre, with the dress. I did not eat her after I killed her, she was too beautiful for that.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I felt sorry for killing her, but by then it was already too late.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, she does match the description I was given, it is her after all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I demand that you hand the body over to me! I must return them to her father!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: You demand nothing! You may have uncovered my disguise, but my claws can still tear you apart at will!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I will not allow you to leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I humbly request to be given the remains, so I can return them to her father.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I cannot do that. If they knew I wasn't a god, and if her father knew I killed his daughter, the whole tribe would come in here at once and I'd be finished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will kill you myself!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Come and try!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't allow you to leave either. You cannot tell anyone about me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you're completely mad, walking around in your silly bearsuit, killing innocent people!

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: And how is that very much different from you, walking around in silly suits of armour, killing everything that moves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's [player name/fremennik name], of whom many legends are sung.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: You are brave, to come into my cave and say a thing like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Slumber? You didn't seem to be sleeping.

lines.speaker: The Kendal
lines.text: Do not mock me, mortal!

sections.title: Trying to pick up another corpse
Trying to bury the corpse before talking to Hamal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the man who murdered Asleif! He was in a cave just by the pool, but I killed him!

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Do not say such things, outerlander! She is not dead, merely missing.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: She'll show up again... yes, yes, I'm sure of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But she's been missing for more than ten years!

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Yes, I know... well, that doesn't mean she can't still come back, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yeah, right... sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, here, I even have her corpse!

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I do not want to see any of the corpses you're carrying around with you! Begone!

sections.title: Talking to Shining pool before Hamal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found him! I found your murderer! It was some bear-like man who called himself the Kendal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it was actually a guy in a bearsuit. But he's dead now.

sections.title: Talking to Hamal about the Kendal for the first time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know there is a strange bear like thing in a cave near the pool?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes, it is the Kendal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only the cave is hidden behind some trees, you see-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, you mean you know that already?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Of course. We planted the trees there ourselves many years ago.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: It is unwise to come too close to a god, so we made sure no one would go there anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But but but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's not a god! He's a man in a bearsuit! And he killed your daughter!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And why didn't you tell me she's been missing for more than ten years?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Fourteen years, to be exact. And I didn't say otherwise, did I? As for the Kendal, what proof have you of these serious accusations?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want proof for everything, don't you? Why don't you just go and have a look?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Like I said, it is not wise to approach a god so closely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I've only gone and killed him, haven't I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I've only gone and killed him, haven't I? I'm even wearing his hat!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You have? Then we made an enormous mistake.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: As did he. Impersonating a god bears the highest penalty imaginable, but I did not think that kind of justice would be delivered by the hand of an outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I also found your daughter. The bearman killed her.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: So it is true then, after all? Let me see her body please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't bring it with me, I'll go and look for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes, I see it is her. So it is also true that you spoke to her spirit at the pool?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it's true.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Then she must be buried there, on the island at the center of the pool... to bring peace to her spirit. This is a most unusual situation, but will you bury her, [player name/fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Then please listen closely. If she is given a proper burial, she is to be buried with some of her possessions. I see she's still wearing her dress, so you will need one more item.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: When she has been buried, you must build a cairn on top. It is our tradition to build these cairns in the shape of our longboats that we use when we need to cross the seas. You can use rocks for this purpose, about five or so should be enough.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Only then are the burial rites complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I need to bury her with one of her possessions, and build a cairn on top made out of rocks. So where do I get another one of her possessions?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Asleif had little possessions in life and none that have survived the years. I do not know where you might find such a thing, but perhaps Ragnar does, out by the pool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... ehm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I killed the bearman! He really was just a man after all, like I said!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You have? Then we made an enormous mistake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help with killing this bear thing in the cave. Do you have any warriors to come with me?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: If you wish to confront the god it is your own affair, outerlander, but we will not be any part of it.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I heard the news from Hamal, outerlander. Alas, I have known this had to be so for a long time, but Hamal just didn't want to listen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh huh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, Hamal asked me to bury her with some of her possessions. But he doesn't have any anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have anything that may help?

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I'm not altogether sure... I do have one thing that may be appropriate.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I was going to give her a necklace, when we were supposed to meet here many years ago.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I still have it, and since it was a gift for Asleif, technically it's hers.

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Take it, outerlander, and bury it with her. It will be my parting gift.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been waiting to give it to her all that time?

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: Oh yes... she is my only love, you see, and will be so forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that's ridiculous! Don't you ever go out and have some fun? What is wrong with you people!

lines.speaker: Ragnar
lines.text: I do not expect you to understand such things. But perhaps you will find your own love one day, and then you will remember these words.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already got a marriage lined up, thanks.

sections.title: Listening to the Shining pool before laying Asleif to rest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhm... Asleif? Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I convinced your father about your death. He asked me to give you a proper burial, here on this island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's kind of awkward... can you maybe be silent while I bury you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, right. Well, I'd better get on with it then.

sections.title: Listening to the Shining pool before building the cairn

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhm... Asleif? Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello? Are you there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Burying her must have really helped! I should get on with building the cairn on top.

sections.title: Talking to Hamal after quest completion

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You are a most unusual outerlander. The council of Rellekka was wise to make you an honourary Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You are a most unusual outerlander. Perhaps you should seek to become an honourary Fremennik, in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You are a most unusual outerlander. I'm sure that you will be able to complete your Fremennik trials some time.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You have my thanks, in any case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you all going to do now? Are you finally going to move to another location?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: No, I've decided to set up a permanent camp here.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: There is no need to move now with our new supply of food. Also, there is a protective spirit looking after us now.
